questions.q: Describe the characteristics of the tertiary sector. How does it contribute to the economy? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The tertiary sector is characterized by services-based activities. It includes services such as banking, insurance, tourism, transportation, communication, and healthcare. The tertiary sector contributes significantly to the economy by generating employment opportunities, increasing income, and stimulating economic growth. It also helps in the development of infrastructure and the creation of new industries.
marking_scheme: Characteristics of the tertiary sector (1 mark)
Contribution of the tertiary sector to the economy (1 mark)
Examples of tertiary sector activities (1 mark)
Importance of the tertiary sector (1 mark)

questions.q: What are the differences between primary, secondary, and tertiary activities? Explain with examples. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Primary activities are related to the extraction and production of natural resources. Examples include agriculture, forestry, fishing, and mining. Secondary activities involve the processing and manufacturing of raw materials. Examples include textiles, steel, and cement. Tertiary activities are service-based and include services such as banking, tourism, and transportation.
marking_scheme: Definition of primary, secondary, and tertiary activities (1 mark)
Differences between primary, secondary, and tertiary activities (1 mark)

questions.q: Describe the characteristics of the quaternary sector. Explain how it differs from the tertiary sector. (5 marks)
questions.answer: The quaternary sector is characterized by information-based activities. It includes activities such as research and development, software development, data processing, and education. The quaternary sector differs from the tertiary sector in that it involves the creation and dissemination of information, whereas the tertiary sector involves the provision of services.
marking_scheme: Characteristics of the quaternary sector (1 mark)
Differences between quaternary and tertiary sectors (1 mark)
Examples of quaternary sector activities (1 mark)
Importance of the quaternary sector (1 mark)

questions.q: Calculate the value of the tertiary sector in a country with a GDP of Rs. 100 crores and a tertiary sector contribution of 60%. (Numerical, 5 marks)
marking_scheme: Expression of answer in correct units (1 mark)

questions.q: A country has a population of 50 million and a tertiary sector employment of 20 million. Calculate the percentage of population employed in the tertiary sector. (Numerical, 5 marks)
questions.answer: Percentage of population employed in the tertiary sector = (20/50) × 100% = 40%.
marking_scheme: Expression of answer in correct units (1 mark)

questions.q: A city has a tertiary sector output of Rs. 500 crores and a population of 10 million. Calculate the tertiary sector output per capita in the city. (Numerical, 5 marks)
questions.answer: Tertiary sector output per capita = Rs. 500 crores / 10 million = Rs. 0.05 crores or Rs. 500,000 per capita.
marking_scheme: Expression of answer in correct units (1 mark)
